channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: The RPI Experience: What’s New, What’s Next Virtual Session
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 25, 2026 2:00 PM - 3:00 PM - Curious what’s changing with The RPI Experience (Formerly The Arch) and what it means for you? Come join us for a live virtual chat where we’ll break down the latest updates, walk through expectations, and help you plan your next steps with confidence. We will also cover key details about Discovery semester registration for summer, fall, and spring, and answer your questions so you feel prepared for what’s ahead!
item.author: Students

item.title: Passover Kosher Meals - Sign Up before March 26th
item.description: Passover is approaching, and we are now accepting sign-ups for Kosher meals for Passover. Please make sure to register no later than Thursday, March 26, 2026. Click the link above for more information and how to sign up.
item.author: Students

item.title: RPI Summer Student Employment
item.description: The RPI Pipeline Office is currently accepting student applications to work in part-time and full-time positions on campus, this July, with the 2026 RPI STEP and the RPI PREFACE programs, mentoring pre-college students. Applications will be accepted until all positions are filled.
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Center Tabling at the Union Every Wednesday
item.description: Meet your tutors every Wednesday, 12–2 PM in the Union. Stop by the Student Success Center table to meet our tutors, grab a Puck Cookie, and book a session on the spot. We offer Drop-In Tutoring at three locations (Success Center, ECAV, and Nason), Small Group Tutoring for upper-level courses, and One-to-One Tutoring, new this semester. No appointment needed to say hi. Come find us!
item.author: Students

item.title: Lally Excellence in Teaching Award Nominations Now Open
item.description: Recognize a Lally faculty member who made a lasting impact on your academic experience. The Lally Excellence in Teaching Award honors instructors who inspire learning, foster engaging classrooms, and go above and beyond for students. Submit a nomination by April 1 using this link: https://forms.gle/hzGhR2Wjih7NoppB7.
item.author: Students

item.title: Leaders in Action: Conversation with Jon Stevens ’84 and Meri Stevens ’84, ’84G Scheduled for March 27
item.description: Date: Friday, March 27, 2026 2:00 PM - 3:30 PM - Join host Prof. Azita Hirsa as she welcomes Jon Stevens ’84, former Chief Ethics Officer at SKF Group, and Meri Stevens ’84, ’84G, COO of Kenvue, for a Leaders in Action conversation on Building Trust, Performance, and Culture in Complex Organizations. Gain insights on ethical decision-making and leadership at scale, followed by Q&A.
item.author: Community

item.title: Shuttle buses running limited schedule for 3/25
item.description: Shuttle buses will operate on March 25 from 9:00 AM to 8:00 PM, with one bus serving the North Route and one bus serving the West Route
item.author: Community

item.title: 4th Science and Technology Studies Research Day
item.description: Join the Department of Science and Technology Studies on March 25th from 9am-3pm in Sage 5711 for their 4th STS Research Day! Members of the STS community, undergraduate and graduate students and faculty, share their work-in-progress. Expect presentations on a diversity of themes, from the politics of data centers, to labor issues in Mexico’s computer industry, and more!
item.author: Community

item.title: Gamefest 2026
item.description: March 27-28, 2026 GameFest is an annual showcase of student talent in game development, bringing together students from around New York State and the Northeast to exhibit their games. The GameFest awards recognize excellence in game design, technical innovation, artistic quality, and narrative design, and are awarded by a panel of expert judges from game studios in the Capital Region. Presented by Games and Experimental Media Symposium Annual showcase and symposium of student game development
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI at Work Information Session Scheduled for March 27
item.description: Date: Friday, March 27, 2026 12:00 PM - 12:30 PM - RPI at Work Dean Aric Krause, Ph. D., will be reviewing the online, project-based graduate degrees and certificates. Expect to hear details about how RPI Instructor Mentors provide industry expertise, advice, and mentorship in a targeted and experiential learning environment. Please RSVP if you plan to attend and learn more about RPI at Work.
item.author: Community

item.title: 11th Annual Graduate Research Symposium
item.description: Date: Friday, April 3, 2026 1:00 PM - 6:00 PM - Register to attend the 11th Annual Graduate Research Symposium to learn about the great research our graduate students are doing here at RPI. Events include podium presentation talks (1:00 - 2:30 p.m.), the final round of the Three Minute Thesis competition (3:00 - 4:15 p.m.), and the poster presentation session and networking reception (4:30 - 6:00 p.m.).
item.author: Community

item.title: The Future of Computing Research Collaboration (FCRC) 2026 Annual Workshop
item.description: This full-day workshop brings together RPI and IBM leadership, faculty, researchers, and students to highlight strategic priorities, research progress across the four FCRC tracks (AI Algorithms, AI HW/SW Codesign, Semiconductor Technology, and Quantum Computing), and future collaboration opportunities. Visit https://fcrc.rpi.edu/news for the agenda and registration link.
item.author: Community

item.title: Department of Economics Seminar: Olga Schurchkov, Wellesley College
item.description: Please join us as the Department of Economics welcomes Dr. Olga Shurchkov, who will present: “The Optimal Size of a Lie: Persuasion and Perceptions in Cheating Games” on Wednesday, March 25 at 3:30 PM in SAGE 3713.
item.author: Community

item.title: Celebrate the Future Generations of Game Designers at GameFest 2026
item.description: GameFest returns to EMPAC on Saturday, March 28 from 12pm-7pm. GameFest is an annual showcase of student talent in game development, bringing together students from around New York State and the Northeast to exhibit their games. This year’s event will feature talks and film screenings from visiting artist, competitive eSports matches, and a retro gaming lounge.
item.author: Community

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Live Course: “The Art of Clear and Confident Communication”
item.description:
Join us for Dr. Mike Bechtle’s The Art of Clear and Confident Communication Leadercamp and leave empowered with immediately actional tools for communicating effectively in any situation. Two times: 9:00AM - 10:00AM and 12:00PM - 1:00PM.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
